name: navbuddy-100
description: NavBuddy-100 GT evaluation split: 100 human-verified navigation samples across 4 Australian cities (Brisbane, Sydney, Melbourne, Canberra). One dashcam frame per sample, paired with OSM overhead maps. Human-annotated labels for lane-change detection and next-action prediction.
download_instructions:
To download images, you need a Google Maps API key with Street View Static API enabled.

1. Get an API key: https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/streetview/get-api-key
2. Run: navbuddy download-manifest --manifest manifest.json --api-key YOUR_KEY

brisbane_route0bmpfsqys_step001.enhanced_instruction: Move to the left lane and turn left onto Herschel Street at the upcoming traffic lights.
brisbane_route0bmpfsqys_step001.reasoning: The instruction is clear and concise, with no landmarks visible that could be misleading. The lane guidance is not required as it's a straight ahead turn.

brisbane_route0bmpfsqys_step002.instruction: Turn left onto N Quay past the scaffolded building on right.
brisbane_route0bmpfsqys_step002.enhanced_instruction: At the end of George Street, use the left lane to turn onto North Quay.
brisbane_route0bmpfsqys_step002.reasoning: The dashcam images show a clear urban street with extensive scaffolding on a building to the right side. There are visible roadworks with traffic cones and barriers throughout the approach. The road appears to have 2 lanes. The scaffolded building is a prominent landmark on the right that will be visible as the driver approaches the left turn onto N Quay. The construction activity and cones present hazards that require caution. Lane change is required to position for the left turn.

brisbane_route0bmpfsqys_step004.reasoning: The vehicle is currently on the M3 Pacific Motorway. While the ultimate goal is exit 8, it is still approximately 7km away according to the map data. The images show the vehicle moving straight along a 3-lane motorway section bounded by a prominent sound wall on the left. No immediate lane change is required for the upcoming distance.
